content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves sharply to the left ahead with the car on the right side of the track, still in the recovery U-turn with heading ~-145° while needing to face roughly east/northeast.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 83, target = 83 + 5 = 88
- Waypoint 88: x=7.0997, y=1.1461
- Car position: x=0.9917, y=4.3580
- Bearing = atan2(1.1461 - 4.3580, 7.0997 - 0.9917) = atan2(-3.2119, 6.1080) = atan2(-3.212, 6.108) ≈ -27.7°
- Current heading: -144.77°
- Delta = -27.7 - (-144.77) = +117.07° → positive = steer LEFT

Still very large positive delta — must continue maximum left steering.

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Car at x=0.99, y=4.36 heading -145° (facing southwest) while target waypoint 88 is at bearing -28°, giving delta +117° — must continue maximum left steering at minimum speed to complete the U-turn recovery.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Car in prolonged U-turn recovery near x=0.99-1.13, y=4.36-4.53 for many steps — heading ~-145° to -162° facing southwest",
    "Track runs roughly east (positive X direction) near waypoints 80-90",
    "Large positive delta (~117°+) requires maximum left steering (25°) at low speed (0.75)",
    "Car is executing a very slow U-turn recovery — must persist with max left steer",
    "is_left_of_center=false means car is right of center — acceptable during recovery",
    "Track width 0.76m — limited margin, must stay on track during realignment",
    "Progress ~69.9% near waypoints 82-83, track curves left ahead",
    "Visual confirms sharp left curve ahead — car on right side of track near outer edge",
    "Keep max left steering until heading approaches target bearing (~-20° to 0°)",
    "Once heading aligns with track direction (~-20° to 0°), reduce steering and increase speed"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [1.086, 1.053, 1.053, 1.026, 0.992],
    "y": [4.485, 4.444, 4.444, 4.409, 4.358],
    "yaw": [-156.236, -151.913, -151.913, -149.104, -144.770]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": 117.07,
  "speed": 0.75,
  "steering_angle": 25.0
}
```
